executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with 104 Canadian organizations compromised in a single day across all sectors. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security has issued an urgent advisory (AL26-014) regarding 'FortiBleed' — a massive leak of thousands of compromised Fortinet device credentials now circulating among threat actors. Most alarming: critical infrastructure targets include Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, Eeyou Communications Network (telecom), and the Municipality of North Perth. Major brands hit include Canada Goose and National Money Mart (database fully leaked). Twenty distinct ransomware groups are actively dumping Canadian victim data, led by Qilin (21 victims), Play (11), and a massive Clop campaign (16 victims).

items.description: Leak of thousands of compromised credentials for Fortinet devices enabling unauthorized access
items.software_affected: Fortinet devices (specific models not specified in advisory)
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a national ransomware emergency with 104 organizations compromised on June 18, 2026. CCCS has issued Alert AL26-014 regarding FortiBleed, a critical leak of thousands of Fortinet device credentials actively being exploited. Public sector entities (Peterborough Public Health, Municipality of North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Eastern Townships School Board) and critical infrastructure (Eeyou Communications Network telecom) have been severely impacted.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose compromised by CoinbaseCartel ransomware; Active Green + Ross compromised by Sinobi ransomware; Kids & Company compromised by Sinobi ransomware
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company database fully leaked by Everest ransomware; Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi; Trucash compromised by Lynx; Axion50plus compromised by Devman; multiple accounting firms (Pewarchuk CPA, MG CPA, Reidhurst Nagy) compromised

report_2w.executive_summary: Two critical zero-day vulnerabilities are under active exploitation: Palo Alto's PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN (CVE-2026-0257) and Cisco's SD-WAN vManage (CVE-2026-20262). Both require immediate patching. The threat actor ShinyHunters claims massive breaches affecting the Council of Europe (297GB, 429k files including payroll and medical records), Infinite Campus (137,000 school staff accounts), and 7-Eleven. Chinese state-sponsored hackers breached REDCap servers to steal medical research data. A Microsoft 365 Copilot vulnerability could have enabled one-click theft of emails, files, and multi-factor authentication codes.

canada_landscape.summary: No direct Canadian institutional breaches or CCCS advisories identified in this period. One upcoming Canadian infosec event (BSidesUme, June 16-17 in Sweden, mentions Canada in event listing). No targeting of major Canadian retail chains (Loblaws, Canadian Tire, Shoppers Drug Mart) or financial institutions (CIBC, RBC, TD, Scotiabank, BMO) was reported.
